metadata.title: Review Case: Methodology Experiment Report Review
metadata.note: Pedagogical reconstruction — not a verbatim review transcript. See .md file for version binding.

translation.note: English translation of source examples/methodology-review.json, using en/examples/methodology-review.md as terminology reference

source_material.title: AI Collaboration Project Full Lifecycle Framework §6.3.2 Flow-as-Node Nested Workflow Control Experiment

D1_logic.anchor: A/B arm definitions are clear and the control logic is correct. The inferential leap from conclusion to attribution is not sufficiently bridged.

D2_accuracy.anchor: Statistical report is consistent with experiment configuration. The facts of temp=0 and n=4 are accurate, but their methodological consequences are not fully discussed.

D3_method.anchor: A controlled experiment is an appropriate method. However, F1 as the sole metric may be insensitive to structural differences.

D4_completeness.anchor: Four gaps: temp=0 generalizability / per-category sample distribution / information-equivalence assumption / precision-recall decomposition.

D5_clarity.anchor: Clear structure, [E-] evidence labeling used appropriately, terminology well-defined.

D6_insight.anchor: Honest reporting of a negative result is rare in prompt-engineering literature and carries methodological value.

challenges.A_alternative_explanations: [MAJOR] Ceiling effects may stem from coarse-grained F1 rather than a true null format effect. Recommend adding precision/recall decomposition.
challenges.B_hidden_assumptions: [MAJOR] Implicit information-equivalence assumption — hierarchical vs. flat descriptions may carry different amounts of information.
challenges.C_boundary_conditions: [MODERATE] Generalization boundary unclear — does the conclusion within GPT-5.5 Chinese coding tasks apply to other models/languages/temp>0?
challenges.D_counterexamples: [MINOR] Do positive CoT format effect reports constitute counterexamples? Recommend distinguishing content format vs. reasoning format.
challenges.E_method_alternatives: [MODERATE] F1 as a single metric — would Exact Match or human evaluation yield consistent conclusions?

post_challenge_score_adjustments.reason: Challenges (A)(B)(E) further confirm the completeness gaps but do not change the score level.
Challenge (E) points out the single-metric limitation but does not invalidate the method itself.

independence_declaration.degradation_notes: Reviewer could not execute the original scoring script to verify F1 values (marked TOOL-LIMITED).

key_challenges_summary: (B) Information-equivalence assumption — the methodological discussion most in need of supplementation.
(A) Coarse-grained F1 hypothesis — recommend adding precision/recall decomposition.
(C) Generalization boundaries — limitation statements need to be clearer.
step4_final_judgment.rationale: The material qualifies in experiment design, method choice, expression quality, and honest reporting. The four completeness improvement areas do not change the direction of the core conclusion (ΔF1=0.000). MINOR rather than MAJOR — the core finding is credible; the improvements are enhancements.

post_hoc_audit.auditor: DeepSeek-V4-Pro (pedagogical example self-audit — real reviews must use a different backend)
